name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “If anyone would like to come out and honor the victims of this tragedy, I encourage you to join us at a series of vigils this evening.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "Anyone who may have valuable information about yesterday’s incident should contact the Anne Arundel County Police homicide unit at (410) 222-4731. If you would like to remain anonymous, please call the Department’s 24-hour tip line at (410) 222-4700.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "I again encourage any individual or family member who needs help to call the Anne Arundel County Crisis ‘Community Warmline’ at (410) 768-5522.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "We honor the courage of the Capital Gazette staff who were determined to press on and publish the newspaper today. They are a tribute to their profession and have quickly cemented the legacy of their lost colleagues.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “We also cannot ignore that those targeted were members of our cherished free press who work tirelessly to keep us informed in a time of increasingly course and overheated political rhetoric.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "The challenge is to find the political will to enact sensible and widely supported measures that will make our communities safer places to live.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “It is inevitable that this terrible shooting will be pulled into the national debate over what we can do as a society to address gun violence. That’s fair and appropriate because clearly we must do more.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “I also want to reiterate my deep appreciation for the swift, heroic efforts of our first responders and law enforcement officials, who are continuing to conduct a thorough investigation of yesterday’s incident. They answered the call of duty with outstanding professionalism.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "And for those who are grieving, we will do whatever we can to help ease the impact of this traumatic experience.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “As yesterday’s events continue to ripple through the Annapolis community, I want to again express my deepest sorrow to the families who lost their loved ones in this terrible tragedy.  We will keep close the memory of Rob, Wendi, Gerald, John and Rebecca.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: Here's my latest statement regarding the shooting that took place at The Capital Gazette in Annapolis:  https://t.co/wBtOn3uwBP.  https://t.co/ItzvlYdwTe

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “Again, for those members of our community seeking information or in need of support, please call the Anne Arundel County Crisis ‘Community Warmline’ at (410) 768-5522.”

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: "Tonight, as the investigation continues, please remember today’s victims and their families.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “I’ve had the privilege of knowing many reporters at The Capital Gazette over the years. I deeply admire their professionalism, their service to our community and their commitment to a vibrant, free and open press.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: “Today’s violent events at The Capital Gazette are heart wrenching. As we work to process this tragedy, we should praise our first responders, whose swift and courageous actions prevented an even greater loss of life.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: An updated statement on the shooting at @capgaznews in Annapolis.  https://t.co/Ehvtnq4PDu  https://t.co/vGlx0oO444

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: More information can be found here:  https://t.co/sDEwp88YLK.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: Annapolis is a very tight knit community, and a tragedy like this shakes us to our core. For individuals or families in need of immediate help, please contact the Anne Arundel County Crisis “Community Warmline” at (410) 768-5522.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: Right now our highest priority needs to be making sure the families of the victims have the information and support they need in this tragic time.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: I am in contact with the federal agencies responding to the scene. My office has established lines of communication with the FBI and ATF to ensure that they have the resources they need to assist the Anne Arundel County Police Department.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
tweet: Our first responders took courageous action today and are managing the ongoing situation with the utmost care and professionalism. We cannot thank them enough.

name: Rep. John Sarbanes
